recent_calls.summary: The user called back to inform the agent that they found another technician and did not need to make an appointment with Budget. The agent acknowledged the message and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to be connected to a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user called to check the status of an HVAC tune-up appointment scheduled through their home warranty. The agent confirmed the user's details and dispatch number, and informed them that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Joe Galvin called to confirm he accepted a noncover cost estimate for furnace repair and requested a follow-up call to schedule the service once the team is ready. The agent confirmed the details and offered information about maintenance programs, which Joe declined. Joe provided positive feedback, rating the experience a five and appreciating the agent's knowledge.

Pamela called to report a leak on her furnace and requested a callback as soon as possible. The agent confirmed the details and assured her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. The agent also mentioned home maintenance programs but the call ended before Pamela responded.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly shortly after the agent greeted the user and began to speak. There was no meaningful interaction or task completed during the call.

The user called to follow up on a service call regarding their AC unit freezing after being off for two days. The agent booked an appointment for April 20th at 8 AM and confirmed the details with the user. The user was satisfied with the communication and gave positive feedback.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called Budget and requested to speak with someone. The agent, Sophie, initiated a transfer to a live agent to assist the user. The call was successfully transferred after a brief interaction.

Anna from American Home Shield called to report a cooling system issue at 5749 Floyd Avenue, Saint Louis, MO. The agent confirmed the details including the dispatch number and phone number, and then transferred the call to a live agent as requested. The call was completed successfully with all necessary information gathered.

The user called to clarify a misunderstanding regarding payment for a thermostat replacement and mentioned having a technician working on their heating and cooling system. The agent acknowledged the billing-related issue and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.

The user called to request a thermostat replacement covered by their home warranty, expressing frustration about the thermostat's malfunctioning temperature buttons. The agent confirmed the service needed and attempted to schedule an appointment, but the user insisted on having the replacement done immediately while the technician was already at the house. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Kristen, called to check on the status of her technician appointment scheduled between 8:15 and 9:15 AM. The agent confirmed the appointment details and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance as the technician had not yet arrived.

The user, Julia from Wansby, called on behalf of Luby Equipment to inquire about an employee named Daniel LaGrace and requested to speak with someone directly. The agent informed her that they couldn't confirm employment details but would pass the request to the team and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to follow up on a work order related to air conditioning at a property, but the call ended abruptly before the agent could gather more details or schedule a service appointment.

The user called to inquire about a work order from American Home Shield for a central air unit issue where the outside unit runs but no air blows inside. The agent gathered the user's information and explained that someone from Budget would contact her soon to confirm service. The user declined information about maintenance programs and expressed urgency due to the heat.

Floyd called to reschedule a technician visit for a bathroom ceiling fan service at his home in Hillsboro, Missouri. The agent confirmed details and noted Floyd's preference for the appointment to be scheduled for tomorrow. The call ended after confirming Floyd's contact number and availability.
The user requested to speak to a customer service agent regarding a work order. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

The user called to book a furnace cleaning service at 4630 Wright Avenue, St. Louis, MO, and preferred a Friday morning appointment. The agent provided information about the service fee and confirmed the user's details. The booking was noted, but a human agent will follow up to confirm availability and answer further questions.

Rosemary from American Home Shield called Budget to discuss a work order involving a furnace and an affected AC unit. She requested to speak with a technician or customer service agent to confirm details before issuing a recall. The call was transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Pamela called to report a water leak from her AC causing damage to her floor. The agent gathered details including the service address and contact information, confirmed the urgency, and informed Pamela that a human agent would follow up shortly to schedule service. The call ended with Pamela acknowledging the information and thanking the agent.

The user, Roy from American Home Shield, called to inquire about an emergency air conditioning dispatch. The agent confirmed the emergency and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Roy from American Home Shield, called to inquire about the status of a dispatch and whether it would be canceled. The agent asked for the dispatch number and service type to assist with the request. The call ended abruptly before the agent could provide a resolution.

Lakiesha called to report a furnace wiring issue preventing the HVAC system from turning on and requested a service appointment for today. The agent confirmed the details and provided available appointment slots, but Lakiesha requested to speak to a live agent, leading to a call transfer. The call ended with the transfer to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a live agent immediately, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about payment options for a compressor replacement service scheduled for the day. The agent provided information about payment methods and asked clarifying questions about the user's role and concerns. The call ended without the user requesting further contact or services.
The user requested to speak with a person named Amy, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The transfer was successful and completed without issues.

The user, Mark Gersten, called to schedule a routine cleaning for his AC unit. The agent successfully booked an appointment for Monday, April 20th at 4 PM and confirmed the service details. The user also mentioned a free cleaning offer from a previous purchase, which the agent noted for follow-up, and the user provided positive feedback about the quick response.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Priscilla from American Home Shield called Budget to request a dispatch for an air conditioner that is not cooling. She asked for an appointment sooner than next week and requested to speak with a live representative, leading to the call being transferred. The agent successfully transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Marco, an insurance adjuster, called Budget regarding a claim about installation issues under their insured's policy. The agent gathered necessary details and confirmed no service appointment was needed. Marco declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the call ended with the agent assuring follow-up contact.

The user called to request an AC service related to a home warranty, describing water leaking from the unit in the basement. The agent confirmed the details, including the home warranty dispatch number and preferred service time on Friday around 3 or 4 PM, and assured the user that the request would be passed on for scheduling. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further issues.
The user requested to speak directly with CG, and the agent attempted to transfer the call to the appropriate contact. The call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user, Kristen Hutchison, called to inquire about the time of her technician's arrival. The agent confirmed her identity and provided the appointment window for technician Cole, scheduled between 8:15 and 9:15 AM. The user had no further questions and ended the call.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to schedule a furnace installation and clarified that it was not related to a home warranty. The agent confirmed the user's role as the homeowner and attempted to connect the user to a live agent for further assistance, successfully transferring the call.
The user called and requested to speak with CG. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the requested party.
The user requested to speak to Amy, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak to Amy, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate live agent successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user, CJ, called to set up a cleaning appointment for two AC units. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a specific person named CG who had previously left a message. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

The user, Mark, called to report an urgent service request for a cooling unit that is not turning on at his address. The agent documented the issue, flagged it as urgent, and informed Mark that a live representative or technician would contact him on the next business day since it was before 8 AM. The call ended with the user acknowledging the information and no further requests.

The user called to inquire about available times for a visit today and requested a callback at their phone number. The call was brief and ended with the user providing their contact information for a callback.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and no further interaction from the user before the call ended.

The user, Anetta Pool, called to make a service appointment for her AC unit that is blowing but not cooling. She provided her contact details, service address, and home warranty dispatch number, and requested service as soon as possible today. The agent confirmed all details and noted that the team will follow up to confirm availability.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after the agent introduced herself, with no interaction from the user.

The user called to check on a heating service ticket and requested that the member, Philips Knights, be contacted by phone before the technician arrives today at 5760 Vivian Avenue, Saint Louis. The agent confirmed the details and assured the user that the team would follow up accordingly. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Diane called to inquire about why her financing offer was marked as declined, expressing interest in a two-year no-interest financing program. She also reported an urgent need for a new air conditioner due to a leak and heat issues, providing her service address and preferred timing. The agent noted her requests and assured follow-up by the team on the next business day.

The user reported water leaking from the bottom of the cooling unit in the basement and requested a service appointment for Friday around 3 or 4 PM. The agent confirmed the details, collected the user's email, and offered information about maintenance programs, which the user declined. The call ended with the agent confirming that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact the user shortly.
